title: fix: preserve trailing slash in direct messaging path sanitization
body:
## What this does

Fixes `path.Clean` stripping trailing slashes from request paths in `findTargetIDAndMethod`, which caused incorrect method routing when service consumers intentionally include them (e.g. `POST /api/items/`).

**Root cause:** `path.Clean("/foo/bar/")` returns `"/foo/bar"` — the trailing slash is silently dropped. This affects two invocation modes:
1. `dapr-app-id` header (line 369)
2. Basic auth header (line 379)

The URL parameter case (`/v1.0/invoke/<app-id>/method/<method>`) was already unaffected as it uses `strings.Index` directly.

## Changes

- Added `cleanPath` helper that applies `path.Clean` and re-appends a trailing slash if the original path had one (and the cleaned result is not root `"/"`).
- Updated both affected code paths in `findTargetIDAndMethod` to use `cleanPath`.
- Added 3 new test cases in `TestFindTargetIDAndMethod`:
  - trailing slash preserved for `dapr-app-id` header
  - trailing slash preserved for basic auth
  - root path `/` does not get a double slash

## Related issue

Closes #7686
